pattern: Repeatedly sweeping the 26-bucket frequency table forward then backward — appending one character per bucket pass — reconstructs the rearranged string without explicit sorting.
hints: The algorithm cycles through all 26 letters twice per pass (once ascending, once descending), picking one copy of each letter that still has stock.
Maintain a frequency count of each letter; each sweep just needs two loops from 'a' to 'z' and 'z' to 'a', consuming one from each non-empty bucket.
Repeat the full double-sweep until all frequencies are zero; concatenate the appended characters.

edge_inputs.description: four groups of three identical letters each
three distinct letters in non-alphabetical order
two distinct characters with unequal counts
characters at extreme ends of the alphabet force correct sweep direction
only one of each letter — single ascending then descending sweep
